notes: Régularisation 2026-07-20 : 0.10.0 (casp audit, livré le 2026-07-19 et publié sur npm) avait été shippé sans trace cockpit — phases_shipped, current_phase, now.md et le scoreboard sont désormais à jour ; log rétrospectif session-logs/26-07-19-001-0-10-0-audit-watermark.md, reconstruit depuis l'entrée de CHANGELOG et le diff réel de f682356, rien d'inventé. Cette dérive (cockpit structurellement valide, sémantiquement en retard) est exactement la classe que la phase facts-layer adresse ; son premier fait déclaré sera released-version, source package.json. Queue : facts-layer ajouté après check-shipped-log et avant upgrade-command (décision 2026-07-20) — PHASE-FACTS-LAYER.md répond à un incident réel du 2026-07-20 sur un cockpit de production : cinq affirmations périmées et coûteuses, toutes invisibles pour casp check parce qu'aucune n'était une dérive d'état. Nouvelle primitive opt-in casp/facts.json + six règles CASP-FACT-001..006 (hash de source, TTL, provenance de méthode, registre de pièges statique) ; zéro LLM, la ligne rouge du README tient. Placée avant upgrade-command parce qu'elle répond à un incident démontré alors qu'upgrade-command est de l'ergonomie — inverser si désaccord. Historique : upgrade-command promu backlog -> queued le 2026-07-18, séquencé après check-shipped-log. next_prompt inchangé : PHASE-CHECK-SHIPPED-LOG.md reste la prochaine tranche produit. Run node dist/cli.js check before push. 0.11.0 SHIPPED 2026-07-21 (session 26-07-21-001, impl commit 0f67e23): CASP-SESSION-003, the first new drift category since rule codes landed in 0.8.0 -- every phases_shipped[] entry must be declared by a session log. THE MAPPING IS DECLARED, NEVER INFERRED: a log opts in with a `phase:` key in its YAML frontmatter (scalar, or a list when one session shipped several). Filenames are deliberately never consulted -- matching 26-07-19-001-0-10-0-audit-watermark.md against the phase id 0.10.0-audit-watermark would need exactly the fuzzy match the protocol bans, so if the mapping would need a guess there is no finding. ADOPTION IS DERIVED FROM THE DATA, WITH NO NEW STATE KEY: phases_shipped is ordered and append-only, so the first entry any log declares marks where the convention was adopted; every entry from that index on must be declared (FAIL), everything before it is exempt as pre-adoption and the exempt count is printed so a green line cannot be misread as 'all of history is logged'. A repo where no log declares a phase gets NO finding at all -- silence, the same treatment migrations get in a project that tracks none -- which is what keeps a new rule from reddening every existing user repo on upgrade. Backfilling `phase:` into an old log that genuinely exists only pulls the window earlier; it can never fabricate a log that was never written, and removing an entry from phases_shipped is a legitimate third fix (a scoreboard claiming less than the record is not drift). SCOPE STATED HONESTLY: this catches the scoreboard claiming MORE than the record supports; it does NOT catch shipping without recording -- the 0.10.0 incident regularized on 2026-07-20 (a whole minor released with no log and no cockpit bump) would still have passed, because phases_shipped was never appended and so offered nothing to test. That gap is the queued facts-layer phase's job. PROTOCOL CHANGE: the session-log template (templates/templates/session-log.md and casp/templates/session-log.md) now carries YAML frontmatter with a documented `phase: <phase-id>` placeholder; an unedited placeholder declares an id phases_shipped does not contain, so the window stays closed and the check stays silent until adoption actually anchors (guarded because findIndex returns -1 there and slice(-1) would have enforced against the last entry alone). THIS REPO DOGFOODS IT from this session on: session-logs/26-07-21-001-check-shipped-log.md declares phase 0.11.0-check-shipped-log, which opens the window at that entry and leaves the 21 earlier entries exempt. Ten new regression tests, 92 -> 102. A read-only reviewer sub-agent audited the slice before commit (verdict GO-WITH-FIXES) and all five fixes were folded in: an isFile() guard so a DIRECTORY named *.md under the logs dir cannot crash the whole report with an uncaught EISDIR (it would have aborted every remaining cockpit under --all); a real diffable expected/actual pair per docs/check-json.md (window entries vs missing ones, not a sentence vs a count); deduplicated FAIL output so a duplicated phases_shipped entry is not named twice while CASP-STATE-003 already reports the duplication; a corrected README trigger condition (silent until a log declares a phase that APPEARS IN phases_shipped, not merely carries the key); and a reverted JSON re-encode that had turned the em dashes in package.json description/author into \u2014 escapes. check --json schema stays v1 (no new fields). NOT YET PUBLISHED TO NPM -- publishing stays a separate CEO-gated act. next_prompt now points at docs/plan/sessions/PHASE-FACTS-LAYER.md (opt-in casp/facts.json + six deterministic CASP-FACT-001..006 rules: prove a claim's freshness, never its truth; zero LLM, the casp lint red line holds), then upgrade-command.
